title: Anvil Launcher Config
description: Configuration file for an Anvil Minecraft launcher (config.json)

identifier.description: Reverse-domain app identifier (e.g. com.mycompany.mylauncher). Used by Tauri to name the installed bundle.

target.description: Output directory for compiled executables/installers (relative to project root). Defaults to src-anvil/target/ when empty.

data_folder.description: Subfolder name inside %APPDATA% / ~/Library used to store game files

java_version.description: Java version to download and use

update_url.description: URL of the update manifest (leave empty to disable auto-update)

logo.description: Path (relative to src/) or URL of the branding logo displayed in the UI

session.description: Authentication mode. 'none': offline. 'custom': client calls MC.setSession() before launch. 'anvil-session': login (username/password/2FA) handled by the anvil-server declared in 'anvil-server'.

anvil-server.description: Base URL of an anvil-server instance (e.g. https://mods.example.com). When set, the launcher fetches the list of enabled instances from this server at startup (the 'instances' field becomes unnecessary), and 'anvil-session' authentication is validated against it.

anvil-key.description: API key generated in the anvil-server web UI (Clés API). Required to query the server: instance list, sessions and file downloads are refused without a valid key.

window_decorations.description: Show native window title bar and borders

window_resizable.description: Allow the user to resize the launcher window

instances.description: List of Minecraft instances available in the launcher. Ignored (replaced) when 'anvil-server' is set: the server's enabled instances are used instead.

id.description: Unique identifier for this instance (used as folder name)

name.description: Display name shown on the play button

loader.description: Mod loader to install alongside Minecraft

server_ip.description: Server IP to auto-connect to on launch

mods.description: Mods downloaded into this instance's mods/ folder during setup

url.description: Direct download URL of the mod .jar

file_name.description: Target file name in mods/ (defaults to the URL basename)

files.description: Extra files (mod configs, options.txt…) downloaded into the instance folder during setup. Always overwritten: the source (usually an anvil-server) is authoritative.

path.description: Relative target path inside the instance folder (e.g. "config/mymod.toml")

url.description: Direct download URL of the file
